column1: Define a vector in ML context;"ordered list of numbers"
Difference between * and @ in numpy;"* is element-wise; @ is matrix multiplication"
What is broadcasting?;"numpy auto-expanding smaller arrays to match larger array shape during ops"
What's the geometric meaning of dot product?;Measures alignment between two vectors. Large positive = same direction:0 = perpendicular:negative = opposite
numpy broadcasting rule (one sentence);Smaller array is stretched along axes of size 1 to match the larger array's shape
What is MSE ?;The average of square of the residuals
What is residual?;difference of the actual values and predicted values

What is Gradinat?;The rate of change of the loss w.r.t. each parameter. It tells me: "if I nudge w by a tiny amount, MSE changes by ∂MSE/∂w; if I nudge b, MSE changes by ∂MSE/∂b." Sign matters — positive gradient = loss goes up if I increase the param.

What is learning rate?; Step size in gradient descent — how far we move along the downhill direction each epoch. Too small → converges glacially (or never finishes in budget). Too big → overshoots the minimum and can diverge (loss explodes).

What is Gradinat descent?;An iterative algorithm to minimize a loss function. Each epoch:Compute the gradient at current params: dw, db = ∂Loss/∂w, ∂Loss/∂b, Step against the gradient: w ← w − lr·dw, b ← b − lr·db, Repeat until loss stops dropping., The minus sign is the trick — gradient points uphill, we want downhill.
